summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording captures an early‑80s Brent‑era show highlighted by a soaring “He’s Gone”, fiery “Truckin’” and crisp “China Cat > Rider”.
review: The audience tape is remarkably clean and spacious, letting the band’s intensity shine through.  Highlights include an explosive Jerry Garcia on “He’s Gone,” a punchy “Truckin’” with a militant snare lead‑in, and a seamless China Cat → Rider transition.  Overall the performance is tight, energetic, and full of memorable moments despite a single weaker outing on “Don’t Ease”.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (15 × 5‑star, 2 × 4‑star) praise both the pristine audience recording and the high‑energy performance; only minor criticisms (Phil’s bass and one song) keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, clean, distinct and ‘in‑your‑face’; drums and keyboards are well separated, with a slight roomy ambience that adds depth rather than detracts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set rides a high‑octane first half (Alabama → Greatest Story) and flows into textbook segues—China Cat into Rider, then an exuberant Truckin’—showcasing tight transitions and sustained momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Explosive, searing guitar work; in full control and delivering some of his most thrilling peaks.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned as a weaker link by one reviewer but also noted for sweet bass moments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Provides vocal scat and solid rhythm support; praised on “Good Lovin’/Johnny B. Goode” combo.
band_member_comments.Keys: Early‑80s Brent is highlighted as a major upgrade, adding tasteful touches and bright tones.
band_member_comments.Drums: Distinct, tight playing with a notable snare lead‑in to Truckin’; overall drum work praised.
